text: Approaching the scene, vehicle against pole, no fire present.
Hi, we're here to help. Stay still for me.
Patient says trouble breathing and chest discomfort.
Okay, we've got you. Can you tell me your name?
Applying pressure to leg area for bleeding control.
You're doing great, Sam. Just keep talking to us.
You're okay, Sam. We're giving you fluids to help with that.
Patient breathing easier with oxygen applied.
Chest rise uneven, assessing for pneumothorax.
Sam, you might feel some pressure here-just keep breathing for us.
Patient says breathing feels slightly better.
That's good, keep letting us know how you feel.
Checking responsiveness, patient alert to voice.
Airway stable but monitoring for obstruction.
Sam, we're going to move you now-it's going to feel a bit shaky.
Backboard secure, spinal motion restriction active.
Pressure maintained at leg wound, bleeding slowed.
Tourniquet not required at this time.
Okay, we're checking your pulse again, Sam. Stay with us.
Intravenous flow steady, adjusting saline rate.
Administering diphenhydramine, 25 milligrams for mild reaction.
Preparing to move patient from vehicle space.
Giving ibuprofen, 400 milligrams for additional pain control.
CPR not required; patient has strong pulse.
Rechecking airway-clear, no need for intubation.
Applying secondary dressing for bleeding control.
Patient says breathing continues to improve.
Good work, Sam. We're almost in the ambulance.
Reassessing: airway clear, oxygen flowing, IV stable.
We're taking you to the hospital now, Sam. You're in good hands.
